title: Ultraprompt V8.3 Main-Thread Routing and Agent Orchestration Effectiveness
requested_scope: Build an execution-ready technical PRD from the architecture recommendations for improving automated routing and triggering of Ultraprompt skills, agents, and panels by the main thread.
problem:
Ultraprompt V8.2 has the right catalog primitives for automated expert routing: 54 skills, 31 agents, 13 panels, 332 capability-graph nodes, 555 graph edges, 50 skills with dispatch_to metadata, and 47 skills with preferred_panel metadata. The current problem is that main-thread routing does not consistently consume that richness.

The actual runtime path is still skill-first and partly hardcoded: route_intent ranks skills only, pathfinder turns only the top skill into an agent or panel path, panel escalation mostly requires budget=deep, and dispatch_advise carries a static skill-to-agent map that covers 20 skills while source specs define 50 dispatch_to entries. This means a user can ask for broad routing/orchestration improvement and get useful recommendations, but the system still relies on the main thread to manually interpret advisory output instead of receiving a durable routing envelope with confidence, agent, panel, risk, confirmation, artifact, validation, and alternatives.

The product should become a main-thread Invocation Director, not another advisory-only skill. It should return the exact action plan the assistant can follow: inline answer, skill invocation, sidecar agent dispatch, panel dry run, or panel execution request. It must not bypass runtime permissions or silently spawn costly work; it should make the right route explicit, explainable, testable, and measurable.

If left as-is, V8.2 will keep passing catalog and graph gates while under-triggering specialist agents, missing panel escalation opportunities, and producing telemetry that proves catalog health more strongly than live adoption.

repo_shape: dist/capability-graph.json currently contains 332 nodes and 555 edges with health.ok true.
50 skills have dispatch_to metadata and 47 skills have preferred_panel metadata.

confirmed_gaps.title: dispatch_advise is stale relative to source specs.
confirmed_gaps.evidence: mcp/ultraprompt_meta.py hardcodes 20 skill_to_agent entries while source/skill-specs.json defines 50 dispatch_to entries; stale extras include cost-audit and db-schema-review.

confirmed_gaps.title: Pathfinder is skill-first and panel escalation is budget-gated.
confirmed_gaps.evidence: scripts/pathfinder.py ranks skills first, uses the top skill, and sets path_type=panel only when budget == deep and the selected skill has a preferred panel.

confirmed_gaps.title: Panel choice is graph-order dependent for multi-panel skills.
confirmed_gaps.evidence: panel_for_skill returns the first may_use_panel edge instead of ranking candidate panels by panel use_when, pathfinder_tags, risk, or intent fit.

confirmed_gaps.title: Router and pathfinder benches do not fully share the production scoring path.
confirmed_gaps.evidence: scripts/run-router-bench.py has its own score/rank implementation; scripts/pathfinder.py calls ultraprompt_index.route_intent.

confirmed_gaps.title: Telemetry separates bench/runtime source but still has producer naming ambiguity.
confirmed_gaps.evidence: scripts/pathfinder.py emit_event sets source twice, overwriting the producer value with telemetry_source.

confirmed_gaps.title: Runtime adoption gates remain weaker than catalog gates.
confirmed_gaps.evidence: audit-invocation-telemetry reports many activation gaps while default enforcement accepts low live skill/pathfinder counts.

confirmed_gaps.title: Main-thread routing lacks a mandatory routing envelope.
confirmed_gaps.evidence: pathfind_workflow can return rich metadata, but there is no required contract that every nontrivial main-thread decision carries dispatch type, panel escalation, confidence gap, risk, artifact, and validation.

confirmed_gaps.title: Triggering policy is implicit rather than generated.
confirmed_gaps.evidence: Source specs carry dispatch and panel hints, but there is no compiled routing-policy contract with auto_trigger, min_confidence, default_execution, release_critical, or telemetry_required fields for MCP, CLI, dashboard, and release gates to share.

sample_pathfinder_observations: Explicit architect request routed to architect as agent-assisted skill with reviewer and no panel.
Routing-improvement request routed to pathfinding-invocation-review; with deep budget it selected cognitive-governance-panel.
Explicit panel-quality request routed to pathfinding-invocation-review with cognitive-governance-panel.
Whole-repo gaps phrasing routed to review rather than repo-review in one probe, showing review/gap/repo ambiguity still matters.

jobs: Add or modify skills, agents, and panels without updating multiple divergent routing tables.
Know whether a request should stay inline, dispatch one specialist, or escalate to a panel.
Ship routing changes with tests that exercise the same path the runtime uses.

users_and_jobs.user: Main-thread Codex or Claude Code assistant
jobs: Receive a single routing envelope that names the recommended skill, agents, panel, risk, artifact, validation, and alternatives.
Use specialist agents when they materially improve breadth or isolation.
Escalate to panels for high-stakes or cross-cutting requests without relying on hand-written intuition.
Get the right specialist or panel for ambiguous natural-language requests.
See why the system chose skill-only, agent-assisted, or panel execution.
Trust dashboard and telemetry claims about live routing effectiveness.
Gate releases on real routing behavior, not only catalog consistency.
Detect stale dispatch metadata, weak panel reachability, and bench-dominated telemetry before publishing.

goals.goal: Make source specs the single source of truth for skill-to-agent and skill-to-panel orchestration.
Give the main thread a deterministic routing envelope for all nontrivial requests.
Add panel-first and multi-panel scoring so panel escalation is intent-driven, not only budget-driven.
Unify routing validation around the production route_intent/pathfinder implementation.
Track live routing adoption separately from catalog health and benchmark events.
Surface route feedback and governed learning without silently mutating routing policy.
Introduce an Invocation Director contract that recommends exact main-thread actions without taking unauthorized actions itself.

non_goals: Do not make every request invoke an agent or panel; low-risk direct work should remain cheap.
Do not let generated or benchmark routing events count as live adoption.
Do not silently auto-apply route policy changes from telemetry; route learning must remain governed.
Do not replace the existing skill/agent/panel catalog architecture.
Do not require Codex native slash-command parity beyond supported $ultraprompt skill routing and MCP tools.
Do not introduce hosted telemetry or remote routing services.

must_have.title: Source-derived dispatch advice
must_have.description: dispatch_advise must derive agent, focus, inline-only, mode, risk, confirmation, and artifact metadata from source/skill-specs.json or dist/skill-index.json instead of maintaining a static skill_to_agent table.
must_have.validation: A new audit fails if a skill with dispatch_to is missing from dispatch_advise coverage or if dispatch_advise references non-existent skills.

must_have.title: Routing envelope schema
must_have.description: pathfinder and dispatch_advise must return a shared routing_envelope containing selected skill, confidence, runner-up gap, execution_mode, dispatches, panel_candidate, risk, confirmation, expected_artifact, validation_hint, alternatives, and reasons_not_selected.
must_have.validation: routing_envelope fixture validates as an artifact and appears in pathfind_workflow output.

must_have.title: Panel candidate scoring
must_have.description: Pathfinder must rank panels directly from panel use_when, do_not_use_when, pathfinder_tags, phase contracts, risk, estimated cost, and linked skill edges.
must_have.validation: Deep and explicit-panel golden cases select the expected panel even when the top skill has multiple panel candidates.

must_have.title: Intent-driven panel escalation
must_have.description: Requests containing high-stakes, multi-perspective, cross-cutting, release-gate, comprehensive audit, or explicit panel language must be eligible for panel execution even when budget is standard, subject to risk/cost guardrails.
must_have.validation: Pathfinder tests cover standard-budget panel escalation and why-not-panel explanations.

must_have.title: Unified router bench implementation
must_have.description: Router positive, adversarial, overlap, and pathfinder tests must exercise the production route_intent/pathfinder path or explicitly compare any lightweight bench scorer against it.
must_have.validation: run-router-bench.py fails if bench ranking diverges from route_intent for golden cases.

must_have.title: Natural-language routing coverage
must_have.description: Add natural-language golden cases for all core skills plus high-risk specialist and ecosystem lanes; explicit $ultraprompt generated cases remain supplemental, not the main proof.
must_have.validation: Pathfinder report distinguishes hand-authored natural-language cases from generated explicit-invocation cases and gates both.

must_have.title: Producer-safe telemetry event schema
must_have.description: Pathfinder events must use separate producer and telemetry_source fields so runtime adoption analysis can distinguish pathfinder, MCP, command, bench, synthetic, and real user origins.
must_have.validation: audit-invocation-telemetry rejects events that conflate producer and telemetry_source.

must_have.title: Runtime adoption release gates
must_have.description: Release scorecard target=all must include routing adoption checks: real pathfinder decisions, live skill invocations, plugin agent dispatch share, Explore share, panel recommendation count, panel run count, and release-critical lane activation.
must_have.validation: Strict release mode fails when real-vs-bench ratio, live skill count, or required lane activation is below threshold.

must_have.title: Governed route feedback loop
must_have.description: Route outcomes must be recordable as accepted, corrected, ignored, or failed, with optional learning candidates that can be approved, applied, reverted, and audited.
must_have.validation: route_feedback creates a candidate without mutating policy until learning_apply validates router/pathfinder gates.

must_have.title: Dashboard routing effectiveness surface
must_have.description: Mission Control must show routing envelope health, stale dispatch coverage, panel escalation opportunities, live-vs-bench telemetry, Explore share, and top corrected routes.
must_have.validation: Dashboard API returns routing effectiveness summary and browser smoke verifies the cards render non-empty data.

must_have.title: Invocation Director trigger plan
must_have.description: Add route_trigger_plan(intent, runtime, budget, constraints, dry_run=true) as the canonical MCP/API contract for the exact main-thread action plan, including inline, skill, agent, panel-dry-run, and panel-execution recommendations.
must_have.validation: route_trigger_plan returns a schema-valid routing_decision and never executes agents, panels, or mutating follow-up work directly.

must_have.title: Generated triggering policy
must_have.description: Extend skill metadata with a triggering block and compile it to dist/routing-policy.json with auto_trigger, min_confidence, default_execution, panel_escalation, inline_override, release_critical, and telemetry_required fields.
must_have.validation: A routing-policy build/audit command fails when source metadata, generated policy, dispatch_advise, or pathfinder behavior drift.

must_have.title: Concrete live activation thresholds
must_have.description: Strict release mode must require at least 5 real pathfinder decisions, at least 3 distinct release-relevant intents, live activation for configured release-critical skills, plugin-agent share >=50% when dispatch total is >=20, Explore share <=35%, and panel proof via a schema-valid smoke or real run artifact.
must_have.validation: Release scorecard blocks when required live skills or panel proof are missing, even if deterministic router benches pass.

should_have.title: Confidence calibration
should_have.description: Replace raw score-as-confidence with normalized confidence plus confidence gap and ambiguity class.

should_have.title: Panel dry-run planner
should_have.description: When a panel is recommended but not executed, return a dry-run panel_plan with phases, agents, expected artifacts, cost, and confirmation notes.

should_have.title: Catalog authoring lint
should_have.description: Reject new skills or panels that omit dispatch metadata, paired panels, validators, output artifacts, or negative routing examples.

should_have.title: Route replay harness
should_have.description: Replay recent route_outcome and pathfinder_decision events against the current catalog to detect regressions or newly ambiguous routes.

wont_have_this_release.title: Fully autonomous agent execution policy
wont_have_this_release.reason: The PRD improves recommendations and routing contracts, but actual subagent spawning remains controlled by Codex/Claude runtime permissions and user/developer policy.

wont_have_this_release.title: Remote model-based router service
wont_have_this_release.reason: The plugin remains local-first and deterministic enough to validate with repo fixtures.

wont_have_this_release.title: Panel execution without confirmation for mutating follow-up work
wont_have_this_release.reason: Panels remain read-only or proposal-only unless a later build task explicitly applies changes.

in: MCP dispatch_advise, pathfind_workflow, panel_plan, route_feedback, and release_scorecard behavior.

scripts/pathfinder.py, scripts/ultraprompt_index.py, scripts/run-router-bench.py, scripts/run-pathfinder-tests.py, scripts/replay-routing-events.py, scripts/audit-invocation-telemetry.py, scripts/release-scorecard.py.
source/skill-specs.json, source/panel-specs.json, source/agent-specs.json, generated dist metadata, and dashboard routing-health UI.
Generated dist/routing-policy.json and any source/generator changes needed to keep main-thread triggering policy single-sourced.
Artifact validation for routing envelopes and invocation telemetry audits.
Local governed learning policy for route corrections.
out: Changing Codex or Claude Code native agent-dispatch internals.
Removing manual user control over panel or mutation escalation.
Rewriting all existing skills or panels.
Hosted telemetry, external analytics, or cross-machine synchronization.
scope.release_target: V8.3 routing-effectiveness release or V8.2.x polish release

storage: dist/routing-policy.json for generated trigger and execution-mode policy.

mcp_tools.change: Return routing_envelope and derive dispatch data from source specs.
Return routing_envelope v2 with panel candidates and confidence gap.
Return routing_decision v1 with the exact main-thread action plan and dry-run-only execution metadata.
Accept routing_envelope or panel_name and return executable dry-run phases.
Record route_outcome and optionally create governed learning candidate.
Include routing effectiveness and runtime adoption gates.

integration_points: source/skill-specs.json remains canonical for skill routing metadata.
source/panel-specs.json remains canonical for panel phase and escalation metadata.
dist/routing-policy.json compiles source triggering metadata for MCP, CLI, dashboard, and release gates.
dist/skill-index.json carries normalized source metadata consumed by MCP and scripts.
mcp/ultraprompt_meta.py exposes routing decisions to Codex and Claude Code.
dashboard/app.js and scripts/dashboard.py surface health and adoption evidence.
learning-queue.py and route-policy.json govern route feedback application.

sequence_flows.name: Main-thread recommendation
steps: Main thread calls route_trigger_plan, dispatch_advise, or pathfind_workflow with intent, runtime, budget, constraints, and estimated scope.
Router ranks skill candidates using route_intent.
Panel scorer ranks panel candidates independently and through skill graph edges.
Routing decision and routing envelope are returned with execution mode, alternatives, and why-not-selected details.
Main thread executes inline, dispatches sidecar agent, or requests/executes panel plan according to runtime policy.

sequence_flows.name: Route feedback
steps: Main thread records whether recommendation was followed or corrected.
Dream or learning job proposes route_policy_candidate when enough evidence accumulates.
learning_apply validates router/pathfinder gates before mutating route-policy.json.

sequence_flows.name: Release validation
steps: Catalog and graph checks run first.
Router/pathfinder natural-language tests run through production code.
Recent route outcomes and pathfinder decisions replay against the current catalog.
Telemetry audit separates bench, synthetic, and live events.
Release scorecard reports routing effectiveness alongside source/package/runtime targets.

failure_modes.mode: Source specs and generated index drift.
failure_modes.mitigation: regenerate-skills, build-skill-index, audit-catalog-consistency, and dispatch coverage audit fail release.
Cost/risk guardrails require high-stakes, explicit panel, cross-cutting, or low confidence-gap signals.

failure_modes.mode: Bench telemetry pollutes live adoption metrics.
failure_modes.mitigation: producer and telemetry_source are separate; release gates exclude bench/synthetic from live thresholds.

failure_modes.mode: Route feedback overfits a single bad correction.
failure_modes.mitigation: Learning candidates require repeated evidence or explicit approval, validation gates, and reversible application.

failure_modes.mode: Router bench passes while runtime path regresses.
failure_modes.mitigation: Bench must call production route_intent/pathfinder and report divergence if any helper scorer is retained.
Skill instructions and CLAUDE.md require routing envelope use for nontrivial or ambiguous Ultraprompt tasks; dashboard tracks ignored/corrected outcomes.

failure_modes.mode: Ambiguous requests oscillate between review, architect, repo-review, and pathfinding-invocation-review.
failure_modes.mitigation: Add overlap-budget cases, confidence gap warnings, and alternatives with explicit disambiguation reasons.

failure_modes.mode: Invocation Director recommends an action the current runtime cannot perform.
failure_modes.mitigation: routing_decision includes runtime_support and fallback_action fields, and route_trigger_plan remains dry-run-only.

rollout_technical_plan.work: Define routing_decision, routing_envelope, route_outcome, and triggering_policy schemas and fixtures.
Refactor dispatch_advise to consume normalized skill metadata, generate dist/routing-policy.json, and add drift tests.
Add panel candidate scorer, multi-panel ranking, why-not-panel reasons, and panel_plan bridge.
Make router/pathfinder benches exercise production routing and add natural-language coverage.
Fix producer/source event schema, add route feedback lifecycle, and strengthen adoption gates.
Expose routing effectiveness in Mission Control and scorecard target=all.

performance_considerations: Routing envelope generation must remain fast enough for pre-dispatch use; target p95 under 500ms for local catalog-only pathfinding.
route_trigger_plan must remain dry-run and local-index only by default, so a recommendation can happen before any expensive agent or panel work.
Panel scoring should use precomputed catalog/index data and avoid scanning large repo files.
Route replay should be bounded by time window and event count to avoid slowing release scorecards.
Dashboard routing cards should read summarized telemetry instead of parsing all events on every refresh.
security_and_privacy: Route events must truncate or redact user intent snippets before persistence when they may contain secrets or private repo details.
No route feedback or learning candidate should mutate user repositories.
Panel recommendations must preserve confirmation metadata before any mutating follow-up work.
Telemetry remains local-first under ~/.ultraprompt and must not be transmitted remotely.
Dashboard endpoints must remain localhost-focused and not expose raw private prompts by default.

milestones.title: Routing contracts, trigger policy, and dispatch drift closure

risks.risk: Over-routing increases latency and cost by invoking panels too often.
risks.mitigation: Use confidence gap, risk, explicit panel language, and estimated scope guardrails.

risks.risk: Source-derived metadata exposes catalog authoring mistakes immediately.
risks.mitigation: Add authoring lint and generated fixture updates before enforcing in release mode.

risks.risk: Telemetry adoption thresholds fail on low-usage local machines.
risks.mitigation: Separate warning mode from strict release mode and allow release-critical required-lane config.

risks.risk: Learning policy degrades routing through overfitting.
risks.mitigation: Require approval, test replay, reversible apply, and evidence thresholds.

risks.risk: Runtime-specific Codex and Claude command semantics cause misleading advice.
risks.mitigation: Routing envelope includes Claude command, Codex command, and MCP alternative where applicable.

primary: 100% dispatch_advise coverage for source skills with dispatch_to metadata.
0 stale dispatch_advise references to non-existent skills.
>=95% top-1 and 100% top-3 pathfinder accuracy on hand-authored natural-language cases.
0 overlap-budget violations for high-risk routing pairs.
Panel recommendation appears for 100% of explicit panel/high-stakes golden cases.
Strict release windows contain at least 5 real pathfinder decisions across at least 3 distinct release-relevant intents.
Configured release-critical skills, initially goal, frontend-visual-qa, and pathfinding-invocation-review, each have at least one live invocation or an explicit waiver.
secondary: Real pathfinder events are separated from bench and synthetic events.
Plugin-agent share is >=50% when dispatch total is >=20, and Explore share is <=35% in release-candidate smoke.
experience-quality-panel has one schema-valid smoke result or real run artifact before release.
Routing correction rate trends downward after approved route-policy changes.
Plugin agent dispatch share increases without unacceptable panel overuse.
Dashboard shows non-empty routing effectiveness cards.

acceptance_criteria.given: a skill has dispatch_to metadata in source/skill-specs.json
acceptance_criteria.when: dispatch_advise receives an intent routed to that skill
acceptance_criteria.then: the response includes the correct agent/focus and no hardcoded fallback table is needed
routing_envelope includes selected skill, execution mode, alternatives, risk, confirmation, artifact, and validation hint

acceptance_criteria.given: a skill has multiple paired panels
acceptance_criteria.when: the intent matches one panel more strongly
acceptance_criteria.then: pathfinder selects the higher scoring panel and records why the other panel lost

acceptance_criteria.given: a standard-budget request explicitly asks for a comprehensive panel
acceptance_criteria.then: the recommended path can be panel with cost and confirmation metadata

acceptance_criteria.when: the same cases are replayed through route_intent/pathfinder
acceptance_criteria.then: there is no divergence in expected top skill or expected panel

acceptance_criteria.when: live routing adoption is below configured thresholds
acceptance_criteria.then: release scorecard reports risky or blocked rather than ready

acceptance_criteria.given: the main thread corrects a route
acceptance_criteria.then: a route_outcome event is written and any policy update remains pending approval
the dashboard surfaces coverage, adoption, panel, and correction metrics

acceptance_criteria.given: the main thread submits an ambiguous nontrivial request to route_trigger_plan
acceptance_criteria.when: the selected route requires expert help
acceptance_criteria.then: the response identifies inline, skill, agent, panel dry-run, or panel execution recommendation without executing it directly
dist/routing-policy.json contains matching execution, confidence, release-critical, and telemetry policy

acceptance_criteria.when: goal, frontend-visual-qa, pathfinding-invocation-review, or experience-quality-panel proof is missing
acceptance_criteria.then: the release scorecard blocks or requires an explicit waiver

rollout_plan.exit_gate: routing_envelope and route_outcome schemas validate with positive and negative fixtures
dispatch_advise coverage audit passes against source specs
panel golden cases pass for explicit panel, high-stakes, and multi-panel skill scenarios
release-scorecard target=all reports routing effectiveness and fails strict low-adoption fixtures
dashboard smoke shows routing cards and route feedback produces governed candidates

product: Should strict routing adoption gates block local development releases or only publish/release candidates?
What default threshold should trigger panel escalation without explicit user request?
Should the dashboard present route corrections as recommendations or as a maintenance queue?
technical: Should routing_envelope become a first-class artifact schema in artifact-validate.py or remain internal JSON?
Should routing_decision and triggering_policy become first-class artifact schemas or stay in generated dist metadata?
Should panel scoring live in scripts/pathfinder.py or a shared module consumed by MCP and CLI paths?
How should route feedback identify privacy-sensitive intent text before writing local events?
Should dispatch_advise call pathfinder directly or share a lower-level routing envelope builder?
What is the minimum live-event window for release-critical adoption gates on a local-first plugin?
